cross_disciplinary_pairing.description: AgriSense fuses two disciplines into one offline pipeline: an ONNX vision model classifies crop diseases directly from a farmer's photo, and a local RAG layer (ChromaDB + nomic-embed-text) retrieves matching agronomic protocols from a structured knowledge base. The vision output and retrieved context are jointly injected into the LLM prompt, so the language model never diagnoses blind — every recommendation is grounded in both what the camera detected and what the knowledge base confirms. Neither discipline alone solves the problem: vision identifies the disease, RAG supplies the verified treatment, and the LLM translates both into plain-language, costed advice the farmer can act on immediately.

test_prompts.prompt: Tomato leaves have small brown water-soaked spots turning black. Likely disease and 3 organic treatments?
Maize prices in Kano this month: 45000, 46200, 44800, 47500 NGN per 100kg bag. What's the average and is the trend rising or falling?
